Looking at the image, the bar is now at the right side of the main room, near the dark gray wall structure. The bar appears to be at rows 40-44, cols 34-38 (approximately). The progress bar has yellow=34 and red=4.
Wait, but I moved DOWN 10 from rows 15-19, which should put the bar at rows 25-29, cols 34-38. But the image shows the bar at the right side of the main room, near the dark gray wall.
Let me check the current bar position.
Actually, looking at the image, the bar is at the right side of the main room. The orange part is on top, and the blue part is on bottom. The bar is at rows 40-44, cols 34-38 (approximately).
This is strange. I moved DOWN 10 from rows 15-19, which should put the bar at rows 25-29, cols 34-38. But the bar is at rows 40-44, cols 34-38.
Wait, maybe the bar moved DOWN further due to gravity or some other mechanic. Or maybe the bar's position is affected by the maze structure.
Let me check the current bar position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current bar position
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"Orange: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 15:
        print(f"Blue bar: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White cross: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check progress bar
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Yellow: pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'R':
        print(f"Red: pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, but the bar's position changed from rows 15-19 to rows 45-49. How can this be if the animation only shows 2 cells changed?
Let me re-examine. Maybe the bar at rows 15-19 was not the actual bar, but a different object. Or maybe the bar teleported from rows 15-19 to rows 45-49.
Actually, I think the issue is that the bar is constrained by the maze walls. The corridor is at cols 34-38, rows 15-49. The bar is 5 rows tall and 5 cols wide. When the bar is at rows 15-19, it's at the top of the corridor. When the bar is at rows 45-49, it's at the bottom of the corridor.
When I move DOWN, the bar moves DOWN until it hits the bottom of the corridor. So the bar moves from rows 15-19 to rows 45-49 in one DOWN action (or a few DOWN actions).
But I moved DOWN 10 times, and the bar moved from rows 15-19 to rows 45-49. This is a 30-row change. If each DOWN action moves the bar DOWN 3 rows, then 10 DOWN actions would move the bar DOWN 30 rows. This matches!
So each DOWN action moves the bar DOWN 3 rows. Similarly, each UP action moves the bar UP 3 rows, each LEFT action moves the bar LEFT 3 cols, and each RIGHT action moves the bar RIGHT 3 cols.
Wait, but the bar is 5 rows tall. If each DOWN action moves the bar DOWN 3 rows, then the bar would overlap itself. This doesn't make sense.

The orange part is at rows 45-46, cols 34-38. This is 2 rows tall and 5 cols wide.
The blue part is at rows 47-49, cols 34-38. This is 3 rows tall and 5 cols wide.
So the bar is 5 rows tall (2 + 3) and 5 cols wide.
If each DOWN action moves the bar DOWN 3 rows, then the bar would move from rows 15-19 to rows 18-22 after one DOWN action. After 10 DOWN actions, the bar would be at rows 45-49. This matches!
So each DOWN action moves the bar DOWN 3 rows. Similarly, each UP action moves the bar UP 3 rows, each LEFT action moves the bar LEFT 3 cols, and each RIGHT action moves the bar RIGHT 3 cols.
Wait, but 10 * 3 = 30, and 15 + 30 = 45. So the bar moved from rows 15-19 to rows 45-49. This matches!
So the bar moves 3 rows/cols per action. This explains why the bar jumps around so much.
Now, the white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The bar is at rows 45-49, cols 34-38.
To move the bar to the white cross:
- UP: moves bar UP 3 rows per action
- LEFT: moves bar LEFT 3 cols per action
The bar needs to move UP 14 rows (from 45 to 31) and LEFT 13 cols (from 34 to 21).
- UP 5: 45 - 15 = 30, bar at rows 30-34
- LEFT 5: 34 - 15 = 19, bar at cols 19-23
But the white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The bar at rows 30-34, cols 19-23 would overlap the white cross.
Let me try UP 5, LEFT 5.
But wait, the progress bar has yellow=24 and red=4. I have 24 actions left. UP 5 + LEFT 5 = 10 actions, which is within the budget.
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Bar is at rows 45-49, cols 34-38
# Each action moves bar 3 rows/cols
# White cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22
# UP 5: rows 45-15=30 to 49-15=34, bar at rows 30-34
# LEFT 5: cols 34-15=19 to 38-15=23, bar at cols 19-23
# This should overlap the white cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22

action(['UP'] * 5 + ['LEFT'] * 5)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
